channel.title: Quote on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Quote on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Perl Bareword Error — 'Bareword X not allowed while strict subs' in use
item.description:
Bareword Error — ‘Bareword X not allowed while strict subs’

A Bareword "X" not allowed while "strict subs" is in use error occurs when you use an unquoted identifier that Perl interprets as a bareword string. This is enforced by the strict pragma.

Description

In Perl, a “bareword” is a plain word that isn’t prefixed with a sigil ($, @, %) and isn’t a reserved keyword. Under strict subs, barewords are not automatically treated as strings. This error is a compile-time error that catches potential typos and missing quotes.
